titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 568

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=878 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 15:48:09.62284+00 | translation moa=879 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 15:48:09.636229+00

p.cl: Dauid dei gratia Rex Scottorum . Omnibus per regnum suum in scotia et lodoneio constitutis scottis et anglis  salutem .

Sciatis me concessisse et dedisse deo et sancto
Cvthberto et monachis eius de dunelmo in elemosinam has terras in lodoneio . scilicet Coldingeham . Aldecambes . Lumesdene . Ristun . Renintune . Swinewde .
Prenegest . Eitun . et aliam Eitun . et Crammesmutham . Lambertun . et aliam Lambertun . Paxtun . Fiswic . et Swintun . Omnes has superscriptas terras . ego do et
concedo deo et Sancto predicto et Monachis eius de Dunelmo . Cum saca et Socna . et toll . et team . et Infangethef . et cum omnibus terris . et siluis . et aquis . et frac
turis nauuium . et cum omnibus consuetudinibus liberas et quietas ab omni opere et seruitio . pro salute animę meę . et filii mei Henrici . et pro animabus patris et matris meę .
et fratrum et sororum mearum . Precipio etiam et defendo ne aliquis de hac mea donatione aliquam iniuriam uel molestiam aut calumpniam monachis sancti Cuth
berti de Dunelmo amodo faciat . quia uolo ut hęc mea Elemosina libera et quieta ab omni calumpnia in perpetuum remaneat .

Hec carta firmata est anno
ab incarnatione domini . m . c . xxvi . tercio anno regni mei . apud pebles .

teste . et concessu Henrici filii mei . et isti alii sunt inde testes . Iohannes episcopus . Robertus
de brus . Herebertus cancellarius . Ascelinus archidiaconus . Paganus de Braiosa . Hugo brito . Berengarius ingania . Gospatricus uicecomes . Aimarvs .
David by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all throughout his kingdom settled in Scotia and Lothian, Gael and English, greeting.

Know me to have conceded, and given in alms, to God, Saint Cuthbert and his monks of Durham, these lands in Lothian, that is to say, Coldingham, Old Cambus, Lumsdaine, Reston, Renton, Swinewood, Prenderguest, Ayton and the other Ayton, and Cramsmoo, Lamberton and the other Lamberton, Paxton, Fishwick, and Swinton. All these lands written above I give and concede to God, and to the aforesaid saint and to his monks of Durham, with sake and soke, toll and team, and infangtheif, and with all lands, woods, waters, and shipwrecks, and with all customs, free and quit from every work and service, for the salvation of my own soul and of my son Henry, and for the souls of my father and mother, and my brothers and sisters. I also enjoin and forbid that anyone cause any injustice, trouble, or challenge in respect of this my donation to the monks of Saint Cuthbert of Durham henceforward, since I will that this my alms remain free and quit from every claim for ever.
This charter has been made firm in the year 1126 after the Lord's incarnation, in the third year of my reign, at Peebles,

by the witness and consent of Henry my son. And these others are witnesses of it: John the bishop, Robert de Brus, Herbert the chancellor, Ascelin the archdeacon, Payn de Briouze, Hugh Brett, Berengar Engaine, Cospatric the sheriff, Aimer.
